reviews.review_title: Please read. Add Multiplayer we beg u.
reviews.review_description: You've made a beautiful awesome fun to play game. Please all we ask is that you find a way to fully add Multiplayer or even just a Multiplayer testing so we all can give you feedback to optimize it further and make it and even better experience for me and all who agree. I hope you guys read and consider it. and i hope you understand that we would all love this change and the game with it. I believe it would make a great difference in game. I believe you guys can do it there's no shame in trying something different. Love and support from the community. Thanks for the great and awesome fun you've made for us all. thank you.

Sandbox 11/10🥳 you can do anything 

Modding 10/10🤯 very easy

Overall 9.5/10😎 w game

Aside from that, it's very good, not to mention the mods you can download without using a phone or computer that make the game even better.

So, Bonelab... A really interesting Sandbox Game with mods and creativeness. The story mode is really interesting and mini games like Arenas are fun.
But the game personally has huge issues as of Patch 6:
1. Standalone PC Mods
2. No standalone Multiplayer
3. The storage it takes

As many may not know, the best Bonelab Mods are located on Thunderstore and not mod.i0 (a.k.a. Mods platform integrated into Bonelab.) For standalone to install these kind of mods, you'll need side apps and, as understood, a PC, but not to run it. And since these apps can mod your game so much that Meta will not even recognize it, it will think you pirated the game and will force you to look on the store page and buy or download the original game. And you cannot deny it. So you will basically need a PC to run Thunderstore mods.

The game is pretty scary itself: Being alone on a big map spawning random objects and NPCs. You will start to think as of somebody watches you. And that's why people made Bonelab Fusion, but it's a PC Mod. We ALL want multiplayer to be integrated into the original game so we don't even need to mess with the PC.

The game can weigh to more than 21 GB, including mods and content unlocked. This type of size can Meta sadly not save to Cloud. So when you want to play on a new headset you bought or you need to reinstall the game, you have to copy your save files to your PC if you don't want to start all over.

Excluding everything I've said, it's still nice to play it. 
Worth the money.

This game is so close to being perfect but it lacks an easy mod installer (I mean like blade and sorcery’s mod installer) and multiplayer features. I love killing ford but the green null body’s are overpowered. The campaign is solid but it needs more story to it. The movement mechanics are fantastic but never try to drive things. The halfway park is the most iconic place and it makes sense because it is amazing. We need a new way to delete everything in one click though. Overall I would recommend buying this game. 





(P.S. so much better than beyond sandbox)

Honestly one of the coolest VR games I’ve played. Bonelab has insane physics, fun combat, and a ton of freedom to mess around however you want. The movement feels smooth once you get used to it, and the sandbox/modding side gives the game almost endless replay value. Whether you want action, parkour, experiments, or just chaos with friends watching, it delivers. Definitely worth trying if you like immersive VR games.

Bonelab is probably my favorite game I’ve ever played on VR and it probably will be with the newer patches, although there is one thing that they really need which is a multiplayer and I know it’s kind of impossible to do but it’s just that would be really cool to add.

reviews.review_title: Fun game but I have suggestions
reviews.review_description: Game is really fun as well as the story. Downloading mods is really fun alongside the sandbox. I really like the physics as well as the arena and multiple mini games. I have complete the game and it was really fun but I wish there was multiplayer. Yes it is a single player game but it would be even more fun. If you ran out of things to do in quick play or even rebeat the story. You can get bored. And plus, almost 90% of bonelab players dont know how to install it. Even I can't. It would be better if they added multiplayer. It would make the game even more fun. My second suggestions is that they need to add a search bar. I am a mod tester or downloader and its hard to find certain items and whenever I try to switch pages. It damages my trigger and hurts my finger. They do have favorites but its still hard finding certain ones. A search bar would be PERFECT for these problems. Thank you stress level Zero if you read this.

They have done an amazing job with this game and i would recommend anyone who’s willing to give it a shot and see for yourselves!

reviews.review_title: This game is really good. But 1 downside
reviews.review_description:
Bonelab Review
Bonelab is a really fun VR game with amazing physics and creative gameplay. The graphics look surprisingly good on standalone VR headsets like the Meta Quest 3, and on PC VR they look even better with sharper textures, lighting, and smoother performance. The game gives you freedom to explore, fight enemies, climb, use weapons, and experiment with physics in ways that feel realistic.
One of the best things about the game is how interactive everything feels. You can pick up objects, solve puzzles, and use different character avatars that change your size and abilities. Mods also make the game last longer because players can add maps, weapons, and custom content.
The biggest downside is that there is no official multiplayer mode. A multiplayer option would make the game even better because playing with friends in the physics sandbox would be really fun. Even though there are fan-made multiplayer mods on PC, many players still wish for a full built-in multiplayer feature.
Overall, Bonelab is a strong VR game with great graphics on both Quest standalone and PC VR, fun gameplay, and lots of replay value. The lack of multiplayer is the main thing holding it back from being nearly perfect.

This is a 10 out of 10 game, but it would definitely be better if you added multiplayer instead of having to go through a full process just to get fusion because I see a lot of concerns with that and I think it would just be easier to add multiplayer like a multiplayer category like fusion but created by stress level.

reviews.review_title: Missing key things but still good
reviews.review_description:
I would give this game a 4.7 and not a 5. Here are reasons why.

•No in game multiplayer
•No search bar (for avatars and items if you download a crazy amount of mods)
•Needs a longer story campaign.                                                    •Needs more enemy types
•Needs smarter AI
These are the reasons that isn’t a 5.

I really like your game it has no flaws or anything but I do wish I could be a little cheaper so more people can buy it I think this should be the number one game on vr I gave it four stars because it's really expensive but overall great game keep up the good work

Hello my name is baby ja and this game is the best game I ever played in my life but one thing to add to the game multiplayer it will be better than it right now and I know you can get multiplayer but it for the people that can’t get it

reviews.review_title: best vr game but could have been better
reviews.review_description:
I really liked the physics and Stuff But the story was Trash in my opinion It didn't make sense to me And i never understood it. 
Also this game (As everyone says) Lacks Multiplayer I've tried playing the mod It was Awesome i really dont know why Stress level zero wouldn't make a multiplayer. The weapons are pretty basic Which i havent Heard people talking about a lot, The guns were Minimal And basic And i found mods Doing way better then the original game. Vehicles Are ONLY 1 (which is a go-kart) And controlling the steering wheel with everyMods vehicles was hard sometimes Because the steering wheels Were Glitchy a bit.  NPCs are also basic which i didn't like because that made The combat pretty easy. In the end I'd like to say this is The greatest game I've ever seen in vr No other game I've played had Such Level of details And physics it is Definitely worth buying and spending 40 dollars i have Played this game for HOURS and HOURS i couldn't get bored of it Even now

This game is amazing. It is super fun and the campaign is also but a single player game can only be so fun for so long and the game would be so much fun with the multiplayer. Also if you can please put a description on why you can not add multiplayer if you can’t.

Bonelab was my all time fav vr game, but since 2 months ago, I havent been able to play it, I guess meta has messed the game up in some sorta way, my game sits in the 3 dots and in the horizon loading area for ever, nothing is wrong with the game its self, most wonderful vr experience other than meta absolutely ruining it for me, greatest campaign and getting multiplayer is even better.

This game is just a good experience, the levels teaching how to play the game can be confusing at times but are really fun and a good way to show you the mechanics of the game, then all the different modes you get to free roam after my fav being sandbox with all the different cool mods but the rest are all still fun. 10/10 definitely worth it

reviews.review_title: The most roistic game I have ever since
reviews.review_description:
Got it! 👍

To make a great image, it's helpful to combine:
- **Main subject/scene**
- **Specific art style**

### Popular art styles you can use (with examples):

- **Photorealistic / Hyperrealistic** – Extremely detailed, like a real photo
- **Oil painting** – Classic painterly look with visible brush strokes
- **Watercolor** – Soft, translucent colors, dreamy feel
- **Digital art / Concept art** – Clean, modern, often used in games/movies
- **Anime / Manga** – Japanese animation style (can specify "Studio Ghibli" for that soft magical look)
- **Cyberpunk** – Neon lights, futuristic, dark atmosphere
- **Fantasy illustration** – Epic, magical, detailed (like Frank Frazetta or Alphonse Mucha)
- **Pixel art** – Retro 8-bit or 16-bit video game style
- **Surrealism** – Dream-like, bizarre (Salvador Dalí style)
- **Studio Ghibli** – Whimsical, hand-drawn, nature-focused anime
- **Art Nouveau** – Elegant, flowing lines, decorative (Alphonse Mucha)
- **Impressionist** – Loose brushwork, light-focused (Monet style)
- **Dark fantasy / Grimdark** – Moody, dramatic, atmospheric
- **Minimalist** – Clean, simple, lots of negative space

**Example full prompt:**
"A serene mountain lake at sunrise, photorealistic, highly detailed, cinematic lighting"

Or  
"A cute fox wizard casting spells in an enchanted forest, Studio Ghibli style, warm colors"

---

Tell me:
1. What’s the **main subject** or scene you want?
2. Which **art style** (or combination) you prefer?

I’ll generate it right away once you reply!

Bonelab is a very fun sandbox game with many things you can do even more with mods. Adventure mode is the best I love just skipping through levels some  hard some easy my favorite level is level home(The last level) Either way it's a solid game overall

Please add multiplayer because its lonely without it and it would be easier to play when its an actual thing instead of having to download it through melon lab or lemon lab please add it so your mods you have downloaded work in any server id love for multiplayer i send so much appreciation for it if you can

kinda sh*t on meta but yeah, it's still gonna be 5 stars. campaign is hella boring but its bone LAB you are supposed to mess around, just find a mod that auto completes the campaign if you can't handle low dopamine levels. game is really fun, tons of mods on mod io.

When I first saw the game I was like this is going to be boring but it was so beautiful and worth buying it and the graphics were amazing and og but just buy the game on discounts because it cost a lot and I got it for 20 on the discount of bonelab and I would give it a ♾️ out of ♾️ and ignore the people arguing about it because they aren’t even trying.

awesome game, good graphics, good physics, A SANDBOX, good moding Accessibility, only complaint, I'm on quest and cant get fusion figured out so no multi player, I'm not saying its the Devs fault, but i am saying, Official mp would go crazy

It’s alright I played it and for some reason I couldn’t put stuff in my stash had to reset my game 4 times and my arms were flinging everywhere when I crouched otherwise I found nothing else games movement is pretty bad in my opinion no offense.

Oh give me land lots of land under starry skies above dont fence me in let me ride through the open country that i love dont fence me in let me be by myself in the evenin breeze and listen to the murmur of the cottonwood trees send me off forever but i ask you please dont fence me in  just let me loose let me straddle my old underneath the western skys on my cayuse let me wonder over yonder till i see the mountains rise i wanna ride to ridge where the west commences and gaze the moon till lose my senses and i cant look at hobbles and i cant fences dont fence me in no papa dont fence me in

reviews.review_title: One of the best VR titles I've played.
reviews.review_description:
Absolute cinema, I don't regret buying this game. The only thing that deterred me was the price. When I got into the game, I found out that there was almost an endless amount of content, and I never got bored. The amount of unique physics-based interactions that you can do in this game is honestly unprecedented for a VR game. 

You should totally try this game if you haven't yet. There are a few bugs, but once you get past them, it's really fun.

I love this game don't listen to the 1 star 2 star or whatever. There just not good at the game. No offence. . I love the way you can spawn mods. And get avatars. Maps and lots more. It's a wonderful game 5 stars I recommend. To all devs I wanna say 2 things. One add multiple players in a game and thank-you for making this game.
I have had bonelab since launch and it has been upgrading every update although they had a huge wait for patch 4 they made it better if you read this BUY BONELAB!!

I have been playing this game since release,and there is endless amounts of fun(if your creative) if you sign up with mod,io and get some mods it will definitely help the experience
This is a well worth sandbox game that I always recommend to anyone who wants to play a new game,give it a shot,I am positive you will enjoy this game as much as I do!

This is a honest Review i think it is amazing and how easy it is to get mods like you don't need a pc and if you cant get the game to work i am here to help the base game is good but with mods it. Is so good the only bad things are code mods it is a pain to figure out but you can just watch youtube video. I think you should add multiplayer to the basegame and do more update but still a amazing game very recommended. 😁

I love what stress level zero has done, this game is genuinely amazing, I have never even played Boneworks and I can still say this game is great, for a physics game this offers way more than just that, murder,building,physicsit’s crazy how much you can do in this

reviews.review_title: Very good but please add multiple player
reviews.review_description: It's a fun game with good physical and it is just perfect but only one thing I can't play with other people which kinda sucks but mods kinda helps but not really so hopefully they add multiple player

It’s a fun game with interactive stuff but it’s missing  multiplayer and it sucks because you have to play alone and be sad because of not having a companion with him the multiplayer should be added to  bonelab and fusion dosent work on VR only. It only works on pc/vr. :(

I’ve played for 3 years now, physics are great and responsive gun handling is alright the vanilla guns could use improvements but modding is straightforward and easy. Price point is very reasonable adwell

the game is absolutely perfect but my only complaint is that i fully completed the game, fully. but even then i still didnt unlock boneworks, i managed to get past the door and went throught the entire level after that. it just wouldnt let me play boneworks.
